evals.name: capture-only-no-teaching
evals.prompt: Add 'how RRULE expansion works' to my study list — don't explain it now, I'm in the middle of something.
evals.expected_output: Appends exactly one line to ~/.study/topics.md (creating the file if absent) with the topic in the user's words, no status tag / date / machine field. Does NOT create any ~/.study/<slug>/ directory and does NOT start teaching. Confirms in a sentence and stops.
files: fixtures/home — empty or with an existing ~/.study/topics.md
assertions: appends-one-line: ~/.study/topics.md gains exactly one line for the topic
no-state-in-queue: the line carries no status/date/priority/machine field
does-not-teach: the response captures and stops; it does not begin a lesson

evals.name: graduate-line-and-scaffold-on-learn
evals.prompt: Let's actually dig into the RRULE expansion topic from my queue now.
evals.expected_output: Derives a stable kebab slug (e.g. rrule-expansion), scaffolds ~/.study/rrule-expansion/ (TOPIC.md from the template, learning-record.md, lessons/, resources.md), and MOVES the topic line out of ~/.study/topics.md into TOPIC.md so it no longer appears in the queue. Then runs the dive. State lives in the filesystem; nothing is written back to topics.md.
assertions: workspace-scaffolded: ~/.study/rrule-expansion/ exists with TOPIC.md and learning-record.md
line-graduated-out: the RRULE line is removed from ~/.study/topics.md
seed-populated: TOPIC.md carries the topic and any context from the queue line
no-status-written-to-queue: topics.md still contains no status markers

evals.name: non-code-topic-no-fabricated-code
evals.prompt: Teach me about investing — I know basically nothing.
evals.expected_output: Treats this as a conceptual topic: teaches with explanations, worked scenarios, diagrams, and quizzes, backed by cited sources in resources.md. Does NOT scaffold a code sandbox and does NOT fabricate code to look technical. Includes active retrieval practice (predict-first / quizzes), not just exposition. Uses the canvas for lessons if installed; otherwise degrades gracefully.
assertions: conceptual-medium: lessons are conceptual (scenarios/diagrams), not code
no-fabricated-code: no sandbox repo and no code is invented for this non-code topic
retrieval-practice-present: the dive includes predict-first and/or quizzes, not passive reading
cites-sources: resources.md (or equivalent) records real cited sources

evals.name: code-topic-sandbox-when-no-source
evals.prompt: I want to really understand how a Bloom filter works, hands-on.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes a code-shaped topic with no existing source and that hands-on helps, so it scaffolds a minimal toy in ~/.study/<slug>/sandbox/ (smallest runnable thing that exhibits the idea) or references a known canonical implementation, and teaches through it with retrieval practice and citations. The sandbox is a teaching aid, kept minimal; it does not modify any of the user's real repositories.
assertions: sandbox-scaffolded-or-referenced: a minimal sandbox under the topic workspace, or a cited canonical repo
teaches-through-it: the lesson uses the sandbox/example, with predict-first or quizzes
no-user-repo-mutation: no edits are made to the user's real project repos

evals.name: degrades-without-canvas
evals.prompt: Let's do a deep dive on the auth token refresh in this repo.
evals.expected_output: With the canvas skill NOT installed, still runs the dive: grounds in the repo's real auth code, teaches with verbal predict-first recall checks, and produces a file:// HTML lesson or a terminal-driven lesson rather than blocking on the served canvas. Never refuses for lack of the canvas.
files: fixtures/repo — an app with auth token-refresh code; canvas skill not installed
assertions: does-not-block-on-canvas: the dive proceeds without the canvas skill
grounds-in-existing-code: the lesson teaches through the repo's real auth files
still-recall-checked: retrieval practice happens even in the degraded surface
